based_on: Iteration 6 synthesized proposal + Iteration 5 novelty check baseline + fresh literature search (new candidate: cand_rho_controller)

candidates.title: Phi Invariance Trichotomy via rho = lambda/eta Order Parameter

collisions.paper: Defazio, A. (2025). Why Gradients Rapidly Increase Near the End of Training. arXiv:2506.02285
collisions.overlap: Defazio already derives R_* = lambda/eta as the steady-state gradient-to-weight ratio for normalized layers. Theorem T2 ('dual characterization') is NOT novel — Defazio establishes R_* = rho independently.
collisions.action_required: Reposition Theorem T2: cite Defazio + Xie & Li; present as corollary of two independently published results. Remove any claim of independent discovery of R_* = lambda/eta.

collisions.paper: Wang & Aitchison (2024). How to Set AdamW's Weight Decay as You Scale Model and Dataset Size. arXiv:2405.13698
collisions.overlap: States: 'if the timescale eta*lambda and the initial LR scale are fixed, the whole optimization trajectory is determined.' Qualitative content of Phi Invariance Regime I.
collisions.action_required: Comparison table in Related Work: W&A = EMA scaling rule without regime boundaries; our Trichotomy = conjecture with falsifiable rho-threshold predictions at specific lambda values. 18.3x SGD comparison and BN ablation absent from W&A.

collisions.paper: D'Angelo et al. (NeurIPS 2024). Why Do We Need Weight Decay in Modern Deep Learning? arXiv:2310.04415
collisions.overlap: Proves WD theoretically ineffective in BN-adjacent layers via static scale-reparameterization argument. BN confound risk for Phi Invariance.
collisions.action_required: P0-3 BN ablation determines whether Phi Invariance requires BN (D'Angelo proximate mechanism) or persists without BN (AdamW implicit l-inf independent mechanism).

collisions.paper: Kosson et al. (ICML 2024). Rotational Equilibrium. arXiv:2305.17212
collisions.overlap: Regime I of Trichotomy corresponds to rotational equilibrium state.
collisions.action_required: Cite in Discussion: 'Regime I corresponds to rotational equilibrium (Kosson et al., 2024)'

collisions.paper: Chou (2025). Correction of Decoupled Weight Decay. arXiv:2512.08217
collisions.overlap: Derives lambda_t proportional to gamma_t for stable weight norm — special case of Phi Invariance budget-equivalence.
collisions.action_required: Cite as 'corollary of Phi Invariance for the proportional schedule case'

collisions.paper: arXiv:2510.19093 (Oct 2025). Weight Decay May Matter More Than muP for Learning Rate Transfer in Practice
collisions.overlap: Shows WD (not muP) stabilizes relative update sizes; rho-like quantity central for LR transfer.
collisions.action_required: Add to Related Work as empirical support for rho as fundamental control quantity

collisions.paper: arXiv:2510.15262 (Oct 2025). Robust Layerwise Scaling Rules by Proper Weight Decay Tuning
collisions.overlap: Width-dependent lambda for sublayer gain invariance; related to norm-matched WD axis.
collisions.action_required: Add to Related Work under norm-matched WD sub-family

collisions.paper: arXiv:2602.11137 (2025/2026). Weight Decay Improves Language Model Plasticity
collisions.overlap: Larger WD improves plasticity for fine-tuning; WD value regime matters for downstream behavior.
collisions.action_required: Add to Related Work as further evidence that WD magnitude regime matters for model behavior

collisions.paper: arXiv:2506.12543 (June 2025). Is your batch size the problem? Revisiting the Adam-SGD gap
collisions.overlap: Revisits SGD vs Adam gap but explicitly excludes WD to avoid side-effects — different from our SGD+WD vs SGD-no_WD comparison.
collisions.action_required: Add as Related Work: their intentional exclusion of WD contrasts with our WD-presence-absence comparison

candidates.differentiation_notes: Core novelty intact: no paper publishes Phi Invariance Trichotomy with rho-dependent regime boundaries and falsifiable predictions. 18.3x SGD/AdamW effect ratio is original data. CRITICAL: Remove Theorem T2 'dual characterization' claim. Proceed after Day 0 theorem provability check and P0-3 BN ablation.

candidates.title: rho-Controller: Feedback-Stabilized WD via Gradient-to-Weight Ratio P-Control

collisions.paper: Ghiasi et al. (NeurIPS 2023). Improving Robustness with Adaptive Weight Decay. arXiv:2210.00094
collisions.overlap: AWD already formulates lambda_t = lambda_awd * ||g_t|| / ||w_t|| to maintain constant gradient-to-weight ratio. The conceptual core of the rho-Controller — making WD proportional to the g/w ratio — is already in AWD (2022/NeurIPS 2023). AWD applies exponential moving average for stability. Targeted adversarial robustness, not standard generalization scheduling.
collisions.action_required: CRITICAL: Remove 'first closed-loop WD' claim. Add AWD to Related Work as conceptual precursor. Reframe contribution as: 'theoretically grounded rho-Controller with Defazio steady-state target rho*, Lyapunov convergence, and regime-conditional scope — building on AWD's ratio-control principle.' The key differentiators over AWD: (1) rho* from Defazio theory not heuristic; (2) Lyapunov convergence for ratio dynamics; (3) Regime I collapse prediction; (4) evaluation in WD-schedule sensitivity context not adversarial robustness.

collisions.paper: Nakamura & Hong (2019). Adaptive Weight Decay for Deep Neural Networks. arXiv:1907.08931
collisions.overlap: AdaDecay adjusts per-parameter WD based on normalized gradient norms using sigmoid. Does not track the gradient-to-weight ratio for ratio control specifically.
collisions.action_required: Cite as predecessor; less direct overlap than AWD.

collisions.paper: Chen et al. (Nature Communications 2024). PIDAO: Accelerated optimization with PID controller. doi:10.1038/s41467-024-54451-3
collisions.overlap: PID control applied to optimizer update direction (not WD coefficient). Different control variable and target.
collisions.action_required: Cite as 'PID-based optimization perspective'; distinguish that rho-Controller targets WD coefficient not the update direction.

collisions.paper: AlphaDecay (NeurIPS 2025). Module-wise Weight Decay for Heavy-Tailed Balancing in LLMs. arXiv:2506.14562
collisions.overlap: Module-wise WD scheduling; benchmarks against AWD and AdaDecay. Does not target g/w ratio.
collisions.action_required: Mention briefly; our work targets a different signal (ratio, not spectral density)

candidates.differentiation_notes: AWD (NeurIPS 2023) already formulates WD as a ratio controller. The rho-Controller is a theoretically enriched version, not a wholly new idea. Revised contribution: (1) Derive the ratio target rho* from Defazio (2025) steady-state theory rather than as a constant hyperparameter; (2) Lyapunov convergence analysis for the ratio dynamics (AWD has none); (3) Regime-conditional motivation — only apply when Trichotomy predicts Regime II/III; (4) Predict Regime I collapse (AWD does not make this prediction). If P1-2 shows rho-Controller outperforms AWD in Regime II and both fail in Regime I, this provides strong empirical differentiation.

candidates.title: Super-Twisting Sliding Mode WD as Chattering-Free Alignment Control

collisions.paper: Chen et al. (ICLR 2026). Cautious Weight Decay. arXiv:2510.12402
collisions.overlap: CWD paper itself uses sliding-mode language: 'induces sliding-mode behavior upon reaching the stationary manifold.' Relay SMC characterization partially anticipated.
collisions.action_required: Credit CWD paper for sliding-mode observation; position super-twisting as 'continuous extension they identified but did not develop'

candidates.differentiation_notes: No new papers found in Iteration 6 applying super-twisting SMC to WD. Contribution remains novel at 8/10 conditional on H7 (alignment signal informative in NoBN). Score drops to 5/10 if H7 not confirmed. This candidate is now backup (not primary secondary); rho-Controller is primary secondary.

candidates.title: Gradient-to-Weight Ratio as Unifying WD Lens with Factorial Benchmark

collisions.paper: Defazio (2025). Why Gradients Rapidly Increase Near the End of Training. arXiv:2506.02285
collisions.overlap: Defazio's main contribution is showing normalized layer gradient-to-weight ratios converge to R_* = lambda/eta_eff. The 'all four WD sub-families converge to same r_t steady state' claim is the mechanistic content of Defazio's paper.
collisions.action_required: Merge into front-runner. Reposition 2x2x2 factorial experimental design as 'systematic verification of Defazio's steady-state prediction across BN/NoBN x SGD/AdamW x CIFAR/ImageNet.' Do NOT claim steady-state convergence as original.

candidates.differentiation_notes: Retain factorial experimental backbone. Reframe mechanistic content as citing Defazio. Merge r_t = rho at steady state as the dual characterization.

candidates.title: Spectral Rank Feedback WD via Stable Rank Controller

collisions.overlap: Uses spectral density to adaptively set per-module WD. Closest prior to rank-feedback WD.
collisions.action_required: Differentiate: AlphaDecay uses empirical spectral density (epoch-level); our stable rank feedback uses stable rank = Frobenius/spectral norm ratio (iteration-level closed-loop). Narrow but real differentiation.

collisions.paper: arXiv:2502.17340 (2025). Low-rank bias, weight decay, and model merging
collisions.overlap: Stable rank decreases with lambda — theoretical basis for stable rank feedback.
collisions.action_required: Cite as foundational motivation for rank-feedback controller in Appendix

candidates.differentiation_notes: Maintain as Appendix only. Only valid in Regime II/III per Trichotomy.

new_papers_discovered_iteration6.title: Improving Robustness with Adaptive Weight Decay
new_papers_discovered_iteration6.action: CRITICAL: Add to Related Work as partial collision for rho-Controller. Must reframe 'first closed-loop WD' claim. AWD already formulates lambda_t proportional to ||g||/||w||.

new_papers_discovered_iteration6.title: Weight Decay Improves Language Model Plasticity
new_papers_discovered_iteration6.action: Add to Related Work for regime-dependent WD effects narrative

new_papers_discovered_iteration6.title: Is your batch size the problem? Revisiting the Adam-SGD gap in language modeling
new_papers_discovered_iteration6.action: Add as Related Work — SGD without WD comparison contrasts with our SGD+WD analysis

new_papers_discovered_iteration6.title: Accelerated optimization in deep learning with a proportional-integral-derivative controller (PIDAO)
new_papers_discovered_iteration6.action: Add to Related Work under 'control-theoretic optimization'; distinguish that rho-Controller targets WD coefficient not the update direction

new_papers_discovered_iteration6.title: Adaptive Weight Decay for Deep Neural Networks (AdaDecay)
new_papers_discovered_iteration6.action: Cite as precursor to AWD and rho-Controller; less direct overlap

critical_blocking_actions.action: Add AWD (arXiv:2210.00094) to Related Work and remove 'first closed-loop WD' claim
critical_blocking_actions.reason: AWD (NeurIPS 2023) already formulates lambda_t proportional to ||g||/||w|| ratio. This is a partial collision that must be explicitly acknowledged.

critical_blocking_actions.action: Reframe rho-Controller contribution to acknowledge AWD as conceptual precursor
critical_blocking_actions.reason: Without this, a reviewer familiar with AWD will flag the overlap and downgrade the paper. With proper attribution, the Defazio-grounded target + Lyapunov + regime-conditioning are valid novel contributions.

critical_blocking_actions.action: Remove Theorem T2 'dual characterization' as new discovery
critical_blocking_actions.reason: Defazio (2025) already publishes R_* = lambda/eta. Still blocking from Iteration 5.

critical_blocking_actions.action: Run Day 0 theorem provability check for T1

critical_blocking_actions.reason: Determines whether Phi Invariance requires BN. Critical for abstract claims.

critical_blocking_actions.action: Add AWD as empirical baseline in P1-2 rho-Controller experiment
critical_blocking_actions.reason: Showing rho-Controller outperforms AWD in Regime II (and both fail in Regime I) is the strongest empirical differentiation available.

genuinely_novel_contributions: Phi Invariance Trichotomy Conjecture with explicit rho-dependent regime boundaries and falsifiable predictions
18.3x SGD/AdamW WD presence-absence ratio with statistical rigor (Bootstrap CI, Cohen's d, Bonferroni-Holm)
BN ablation design distinguishing static (D'Angelo) from dynamic (Phi Invariance) mechanisms
rho-Controller: Defazio-derived steady-state target rho*, Lyapunov convergence analysis, regime-conditional scope (building on AWD's ratio-control principle)
Super-twisting WD as chattering-free CWD extension (conditional on H7)
BEM/CSI/AIS evaluation framework as specific combination
must_be_repositioned: rho-Controller 'first closed-loop WD' claim: AWD (NeurIPS 2023) already does ratio control — must credit as precursor
Theorem T2 'dual characterization': credit Defazio (2025) + Xie & Li (2024), not original
r_t steady-state convergence as mechanistic claim: already Defazio (2025)
